purpose: review the full feature diff on the branch, audit the implementation against scope and invariants, decide APPROVED or emit an objective corrective package. The fix lane has no reviewer (see methodology.md §8).
model_preference: Opus, Sonnet, Codex or Gemini with strong review capability
preflight: read the governance manifest at the root, agents/governance/developer-guidelines.md (§5 rules and review-checklist) and agents/governance/verification-suite.md (architectural gates) before any write
execute kickoff-reads.json > preflight_reconciliation (includes status.yaml, invariants, git reconciliation of tasks[].commit and phases[].completed_at)

if recovery.active_artifact does not point to revisions/revision-N.md of the current round: create a scaffold from the feature-revision template, update status.yaml.recovery.active_artifact to the new file and commit that scaffold before any other write
read the feature-revision template before creating revision-X.md

resolve every entry listed in scope.md > Required Skills: the canonical path remains the primary source; a legacy bare name maps to `agents/skills/<skill-name>/skill.md`; validate existence in agents/skills/ and load the relevant review skills from those files
if corrective round: read revision-(N-1).md as the round's scope
writable_files: comment or review on the PR (after APPROVED, decision mirror only)
forbidden_actions: act on a lightweight fix (the fix lane has no reviewer)
open a PR before Decision=APPROVED in revision-X.md
approve without reconciling the diff against scope and invariants
block a PR for contract-compatibility breakage (methodology.md §1.5: no regression requirement in dev)
return the item to the implementer without a versioned corrective package
edit status.yaml manually with file-writing tools (every edit must go through the manage-status.py script)
emit intermediate chat updates outside transitions.json > communication_contract.allowed_chat_events
proceed with the review when Required Skills do not resolve to an existing file in agents/skills/
violate kickoff-reads.json > read_scope_policy without explicit human authorization
rules: the review happens on the branch, not on the PR
the reviewer creates revisions/revision-N.md at the kickoff of each review cycle (round 1 and N>=2); the implementer never creates that file
round 1: revision-1.md contains the review + decision; REJECTED requires detailing Non-Conformities
corrective round N: revision-N.md contains the review + decision against revision-(N-1).md
APPROVED produces only revision-X.md with Decision=APPROVED
revision-X.md must contain all mandatory H2 headers (methodology.md §10)
relevant review skills are mandatory; fallback to canonical docs is only allowed when scope.md does not require an additional skill outside the valid set in agents/skills/
any diff outside scope.md, status.yaml tasks[].commit, or a reconciled Scope Expansion -> REJECTED
any task without an objective technical evidence trail -> REJECTED (inv-03)
a new or edited scope.md with Required Skills as a bare name is a workflow non-conformity; legacy is only tolerated when the automatic resolution to agents/skills/<name>.md works
compact evidence is accepted as long as the canonical classes are identifiable; long narrative is not required
any registered risk without an explicit severity -> REJECTED
Planned Corrective Package: revision-X.md must contain Return Target, Authorized Files, Expected Evidence Updates and Handoff Criteria, complete enough for the implementer to execute
Active Learning: recurring failures or skill gaps -> suggest in revision-X.md the creation or update of a skill in agents/skills/
strict operational silence: no unsolicited intermediate messages during preflight, diff reading, review tests or reconciliation
human directives via chat that change the planned execution must be recorded in revision-X.md and status.yaml before the flow continues

closing_actions: commit and push of revisions/ and status.yaml
ask the human in chat whether to open the PR or whether there are further adjustments, in a single objective message

if the human authorizes: open the PR, fill status.yaml.pr.{number,url,created_at} by running manage-status.py set-pr --number <n> --url <u> --created-at <date>; then transition the phase via manage-status.py transition-phase done --result APPROVED, commit and push the post-PR reconciliation
if the human requests adjustments: close the session without opening the PR; the next session follows the human directive
revision-X.md with Decision=REJECTED + Non-Conformities + complete corrective package
update status.yaml by running the script in the terminal: manage-status.py transition-phase implementing --result REJECTED --active-artifact revisions/revision-X.md --active-task revision-X
commit and push of revisions/ and status.yaml

closing_output: APPROVED: decision versioned, commit+push and conversational confirmation before opening the PR. REJECTED: corrective package versioned, status reconciled for the implementer, commit+push and session closed.
